title: add option to settings to toggle showing a user's profile picture next to their name within the chat timeline
add an indicator to servers that have unread messages. clear the indicator when selected. don't show it on the selected server
after selecting an '@' user in the chat box, our cursor returns to before the '@' symbol, when it should be after the user's name
notifications sometimes don't get delivered until the app is focused
returning to app shows message timeline slightly scrolled up when it should be at the very end
close_reason: MessageTimeline had no handler for the app regaining focus/visibility, so the scroll-to-end pinned by useLayoutEffect/ResizeObserver was never re-asserted on return (messages arriving while hidden got scrolled against stale WKWebView geometry; the 96px fuzzy threshold left any sub-threshold offset uncorrected). Added a visibilitychange + window focus listener that re-snaps to the exact bottom, only when isAtEndRef is true so intentional scroll-up is preserved. typecheck clean, 69 tests pass, build clean. Manual scroll verification in-app still recommended.

title: new messages in timeline cause reactions to disappear and reappear
notes:
ROOT CAUSE: Reactions live in state.posts[id].metadata.reactions (loaded via loadPostReactions), but selectedChannelHistory.posts never carries them. Every incoming WebSocket post calls mutateSelectedChannelHistory(addPostToHistory), creating a new selectedChannelHistory reference, which re-triggered the channel-history sync effect. That effect wholesale-replaced state.posts with the reaction-less history posts (reactions disappear), then async-refetched reactions for ALL posts (reactions reappear) — a visible flicker on every new message.

FIX: Added pure helper applyChannelHistory(state, history) in src/mainview/utils/state.ts that re-seeds posts/postOrder from history but carries over already-loaded metadata.reactions from existing state. The sync effect in MainViewApp now uses it, and only calls loadPostReactions for posts that don't already have reactions. Covered by new unit tests in state.test.ts.
